tax: Obsidian Slagling is placed at species rank beneath Slagling (Magmatic order, Fire Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a volcanic-glass body, razor conchoidal edges, and a shrapnel-burst on heavy impact. It is separated from its sibling the Magma Roiler by its cooled brittle glass body and shrapnel versus the Roiler's hot molten flow and radiant heat; the two share the genus body-plan but never the molten flow. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: volcanic glass quenched from basalt, kept barely mobile by residual heat.
ap: Obsidian Slagling stands 1.2–1.8 m, an angular glassy figure. Its silhouette is a jagged man-shaped mass of fused black glass shards. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a glossy black conchoidal surface that catches light in curved facets; a dim red residual heat-glint deep in its core, far less than a Roiler's; and razor edges everywhere, so it cuts anything it brushes. It moves with a grinding, brittle stalk, slower and stiffer than the molten Roiler, and shows a faint deep-red core-glint, easily missed in lamplight. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a litter of black glass flakes and fine cut-marks on the floor and walls, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Obsidian Slagling persists in quenched lava margins, obsidian flows, and cooled vent-edges where fast chilling made glass instead of crystal. It does not breed; it forms where a Roiler's flow is quenched fast at a wet margin and frozen as mobile glass, and recurs whenever a fresh quench at the margin re-forms the glass body. It sustains its mass on residual heat in cooled glass, drawing in obsidian flakes drawn in and fused to repair. Range is bounded by the warm quench-margin: outside that band the body cools fully and stops, an inert glass statue. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as an obsidian margin that grows angular limbs against the flow-grain, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Obsidian Slagling is roused by contact or a heavy blow and otherwise lies an inert-looking glass figure at a flow margin. It claims a single obsidian margin and answers intrusion with grinding forward to slash, and bursting into shrapnel if struck hard. Toward grey-folk it is passive until brushed; survey crews report that Forgemasters value its glass for blades but learn that striking it scatters razor shrapnel. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Obsidian Slagling: a body of volcanic glass quenched from basalt, kept barely mobile by residual heat; immune to fire and heat, and to cutting, which only chips it; and a defining weakness — heavy bludgeoning, which shatters it into harmless shards, and full cooling. Its senses run through the medium itself: heat-gradient and vibration through its glass; blind otherwise. It tolerates heat, sulphur, and darkness but fails at a crushing blow and full cooling. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the angular glass body and razor edges, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Obsidian Slagling are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it slashes with razor conchoidal edges on contact; it bursts into a cone of glass shrapnel when struck hard; it fuses loose obsidian flakes to repair edges; and it stands utterly still, mistaken for cooled slag. Each is a direct expression of quenched mobile volcanic glass, so it cannot, for instance, flow, radiate strong heat, fly, or survive a hard crushing blow. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Obsidian Slagling matters because it makes obsidian margins a cutting-and-shrapnel hazard, and crews now never strike a glass figure they cannot identify. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a blade-and-shrapnel hazard logged with obsidian flows, and its presence reshapes routes through the quenched lava margins, now skirted and never hammered. Angular limbs growing from an obsidian margin against the flow-grain mark a forming Slagling.
g: Obsidian Slagling (Vitrum igneum) is a cooled magmatic elemental of volcanic glass that moves as a brittle, razor-edged body and shatters into shrapnel. Documented from the quenched lava margins and obsidian flows of the lower gorge, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a body of black volcanic glass shot through with dim residual heat — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. It is what a Roiler becomes when chilled fast and kept just warm enough to move: a walking blade of glass. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a burst of black glass shards scattered across the floor and no carcass.
